title: CAAS, SAFCo, and Nine Companies Launch SingaporeГўв‚¬в„ўs First Voluntary SAF Procurement Trial
content: The Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS), the Singapore Sustainable Aviation Fuel Company Ltd. (SAFCo), and nine major companiesГўв‚¬вЂќincluding Singapore Airlines, Temasek, and GoogleГўв‚¬вЂќhave launched SingaporeГўв‚¬в„ўs first trial for the central procurement of voluntary Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF). An MOU signed at the 3rd Changi Aviation Summit formalizes this initiative, which aims to aggregate voluntary demand with the national supply. SAFCo, established in October 2025, is tasked with centrally procuring SAF to meet Singapore's target of 1% usage for departing flights, supported by a levy starting in October 2026. This trial serves as a critical pilot to test the operational, commercial, and accounting frameworks for a national SAF ecosystem and the allocation of Environmental Attributes (EAs). For the participating companies, it offers cost-effective access to cleaner fuel credits and credible emissions reduction verification.
author: Analysts Account
